one_liner: GOOG +1.29% to $357.89 as Meta's cloud push validates the hyperscaler AI model — Alphabet competes directly in cloud AI, and its DCF shows 13.3% upside at current levels.

price_action.intraday_story: GOOG opened at $354.61, ranged tightly between $353.23-$359.54, and closed at $357.89. The 0.75x below-average volume reflects a quiet session where GOOG was NOT caught in the chip selloff. Meta entering cloud actually validates Google Cloud's business model.

news_catalyst.headline: Meta's cloud AI push paradoxically validates Google Cloud's compute-as-a-service model
news_catalyst.summary: Meta's announcement that it will sell excess AI compute capacity is actually a backhanded endorsement of Google Cloud's and AWS's business models. For Alphabet, this creates a complex dynamic: Google Cloud competes with Meta for enterprise AI compute customers, but Meta's validation of the demand (enough to build a commercial business around it) confirms the market's size. Alphabet remains in a strong position as one of the three incumbent hyperscalers.

technical.summary: GOOG is in quiet consolidation. DCF shows $412.87 base vs $357.89 current — 13.3% MoS. Not a slam-dunk value but reasonable for a mega-cap. The 0.75x volume on a positive session suggests no breakout impulse today.

wyckoff_narrative: Consolidation without clear phase. Low-volume positive day in a sector that was mixed. No strong Wyckoff signal; hold existing positions and watch for volume.

valuation.summary: 13.3% DCF MoS is modest for a mega-cap. GOOG requires continued Search and Cloud growth to justify current price.

key_concerns: Meta cloud entry competes with Google Cloud for AI compute workloads
fundamentals.summary: Strong franchise, modest valuation upside. The Meta cloud competition is real but Alphabet is well-positioned to compete.

risks: DOJ antitrust remedies (forced Chrome/Android divestiture) are tail risks
Meta cloud entry directly competes with Google Cloud
